type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, unavoidable since both features live in outlines/prompts.py per our task specs. Let's keep changes to distinct regions: I'll add a filters param to prompt()/Prompt.from_str()/Prompt.from_file(), plus a new create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation+filter registration (built-in filters: name/description/source/signature/schema/args, plus any custom filters dict passed in). If your global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) also needs to feed into env creation, feel free to have create_jinja_env() consult your registry too - just let me know the registry's variable/function name so I can wire it in, or we can merge and reconcile after. I'll proceed with implementation now and message you when DONE.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Exact plan: module-level function create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment placed right after imports (before Prompt class). Body: build jinja2.Environment(trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined), register built-ins env.filters['name'/'description'/'source'/'signature'/'schema'/'args'] = existing get_fn_* functions, then as the LAST step: 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)'. Prompt._template_from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(cls, path, filters=None) both call create_jinja_env(filters) (file version additionally sets loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(dir) on the Environment call). Prompt.from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(cls, path, filters=None) pass filters through. prompt() decorator becomes prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) supporting both bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner decorator(fn) closure. For your global registry: insert your merge line (e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTERS)) inside create_jinja_env AFTER the built-in name/description/source/signature/schema/args lines but BEFORE the final 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' line, so local filters passed to prompt()/from_str()/from_file() win over global registry ones, which win over built-ins. Starting implementation now - will ping DONE, then you can add your registry lines on top.
